education_history.degree: Bachelor of Science in Data Science
education_history.notes: Joint program with Arizona State University.

education_history.degree: Master of Science in Data Science

experience_highlights: Built SQL dashboards for business reviews
Shipped a forecasting model for demand planning
Partnered with stakeholders on analytics roadmaps

internship_experiences.title: Data Analyst Intern
internship_experiences.summary: Improved voter outreach data quality and built KPI reporting to support campaign decision-making.
impact_points: Improved voter outreach data quality and built KPI reporting to support campaign decision-making.
Cleaned and standardized weekly voter-contact data to improve data integrity and reporting accuracy.
Built reusable SQL query templates and KPI dashboards for leadership reporting.
Delivered structured insights to support outreach strategy and campaign operations decisions.

internship_experiences.company: University of Arizona - Lung Cancer Research Institute
internship_experiences.title: Student Researcher
internship_experiences.summary: Developed reproducible data analysis workflows to identify effective cancer cell reversal patterns.
impact_points: Developed reproducible data analysis workflows to identify effective cancer cell reversal patterns.
Built end-to-end analysis pipeline (cleaning, clustering, visualization) for multi-dataset research.
Applied K-means clustering to identify stable vs. significant biological response patterns.
Improved classification efficiency by 10% through optimized workflow design.

internship_experiences.title: Data Analyst Intern
internship_experiences.summary: Developed predictive models for earthquake damage assessment using sensor data.
impact_points: Developed predictive models for earthquake damage assessment using sensor data.
Built ML model to predict post-earthquake sensor readings and detect anomalies.
Reduced manual inspection workload by 15% through automated damage assessment.
Validated model predictions against real-world inspection data for reliability.

internship_experiences.company: University of Arizona, James E. Rogers College of Law
internship_experiences.title: Law Library Assistant
internship_experiences.summary: Assisted with legal research and data management for library resources.
impact_points: Assisted with legal research and data management for library resources.
Addressing direct questions at circulation desk from phone call or in-person.
Applied classification and organization principles (Library of Congress System) to manage large-scale collections.
Conducted targeted information retrieval in Nexis Uni, improving efficiency in sourcing case law and academic materials.
Maintained structured information management using ALMA, ensuring accurate cataloging and resource organization.
Tracked and logged service inquiries in LibAnswers, reinforcing structured problem-solving and data entry precision.

internship_experiences.company: University of Arizona, Computer Vision Lab
internship_experiences.title: Graduate Research Assistant
internship_experiences.summary: Assisted with research projects focused on computer vision and machine learning.
impact_points: Assisted with research projects focused on computer vision and machine learning.
Created an image dataset for AI medical model recognition, generating 300+ laparoscopic surgery images.
Designed and implemented a contamination pipeline that overlays surgical artifacts such as blood, smoke, and lens blur onto clean endoscopy images.
Using both coding based method and AI image generators (e.g., Sora, Gemini).

project_experiences.name: Airbnb Relational Database Design - MySQL
project_experiences.summary: Designed and implemented a normalized relational database modeling Airbnb's marketplace operations.
impact_points: Designed and implemented a normalized relational database modeling Airbnb's marketplace operations.
Built an ER model and translated it into 20 SQL tables with primary and foreign keys to ensure data integrity.
Defined 10 real-world business problems and developed SQL queries to generate revenue, risk, and performance insights.
Collaborated on a group project to model Airbnb marketplace operations through a normalized relational database.

project_experiences.name: Cloud-Based E-Commerce Customer Analytics
project_experiences.summary: Developed a cloud-based customer analytics pipeline using Snowflake and Python to analyze e-commerce behavioral data.
impact_points: Developed a cloud-based customer analytics pipeline using Snowflake and Python to analyze e-commerce behavioral data.
Performed feature engineering and aggregated customer-level metrics from e-commerce behavioral data.
Applied RFM analysis and K-Means clustering to identify distinct customer segments.
Generated insights into spending patterns, satisfaction levels, and delivery performance through the resulting clusters.

project_experiences.name: Pandemic Visualization & Analysis by R
project_experiences.summary: Processed and analyzed global WHO datasets across 200+ countries to examine infection and mortality trends.
impact_points: Processed and analyzed global WHO datasets across 200+ countries to examine infection and mortality trends.
Built interactive geographic heatmaps using ggplot2 and Shiny for spatiotemporal analysis.
Applied web scraping with rvest to study correlations between media coverage and case growth.
Analyzed WHO data across 200+ countries to surface global infection and mortality patterns.

project_experiences.name: Online Retail Association Rule
project_experiences.summary: Implemented association rule mining with the Apriori algorithm to identify frequent itemsets and co-purchasing patterns in transactional retail data.
impact_points: Implemented association rule mining with the Apriori algorithm to identify frequent itemsets and co-purchasing patterns in transactional retail data.
Identified frequent itemsets and co-purchasing patterns from transactional retail data using the Apriori algorithm.
Computed Support, Confidence, and Lift to quantify product relationships.
Generated data-driven insights to inform bundling and cross-selling strategies.

policy_result.mobility_strategy: Leverage your US-based Master's degree to utilize OPT/STEM OPT as a primary bridge to employment. Focus on mid-to-large enterprises in Canada or the US that have established internal mobility programs and dedicated immigration support teams.
priority_policy_themes: STEM-designated degree utilization for extended work authorization
Cross-border digital trade and remote data talent mobility
Enterprise AI adoption and data governance infrastructure
constraints: Limited to markets with favorable post-graduate work permit policies
Remote-friendly teams expand the opportunity set
opportunity_signals: STEM OPT extension provides a 3-year runway in the US, making you highly attractive to mid-sized firms.
Canada’s Global Skills Strategy offers expedited processing for tech talent, aligning with your background.
Growth in AI-driven healthcare and infrastructure analytics matches your specific internship portfolio.
policy_result.explanation: Your background in both US-based research and international data science provides a strong foundation for STEM-based visa pathways. By targeting firms that prioritize AI infrastructure, you align your technical expertise with current industrial investment trends. Prioritizing the US for the immediate post-grad period maximizes your OPT benefits, while Canada serves as a viable long-term contingency for permanent residency.

selection_logic: Industry is selected before role so the user can ride stronger policy and market tailwinds.
Scoring combines macro attractiveness, current skill fit, entry friendliness, and visa friction.
Top industries should feed company strategy before final role positioning.
industry_result.explanation: Choosing an industry first grounds your career in a sector with structural growth, ensuring your skills remain in high demand regardless of company-specific fluctuations. For someone requiring visa sponsorship like you, aligning with a high-growth industry—such as Healthcare AI or Enterprise SaaS—positions you at the center of institutional investment, where companies are most likely to prioritize hiring and retaining specialized international talent. By prioritizing the "where" over the "who," you build domain expertise that compounds over time, making you more valuable to any future employer within that space.

discovery_strategy: Filter for high-growth US-based companies with established data-driven product cultures.
Prioritize firms with documented sponsorship history or global operations to mitigate visa risk.
Align company selection with the user's specific internship background in AI, healthcare, and infrastructure.
target_company_types: Healthcare analytics and data infrastructure vendors
company_selection_rules: Prioritize companies where data work directly influences product adoption or operational efficiency.
Favor teams where analytics sits close to decision-makers rather than isolated reporting functions.
Filter for companies with a high likelihood of sponsoring STEM OPT candidates.
ranking_logic: Industry alignment with user's background (Healthcare AI, Enterprise SaaS).
Company stage (Growth-stage preferred for high-leverage data projects).
Visa support potential and international operating environment.
industry_analysis: Healthcare AI rewards companies that connect model performance to clinical workflow adoption.
Enterprise SaaS gives broad access to product, revenue, and usage data with clear experimentation loops.
Data infrastructure modernization is a key investment area for incumbents.
market_analysis: B2B buyers now expect AI augmentation and measurable productivity gains.
Companies with product-led data loops often develop stronger analyst and data scientist scope.
Efficiency-focused buyers reward products that reduce manual operations.
value_chain_analysis: High-value positions sit near clinical data infrastructure and decision support.
The best operating leverage comes from turning fragmented processes into repeatable data products.
The most strategic teams sit between product usage, monetization, and go-to-market efficiency.
competitor_map: Clinical AI platforms: Tempus, Abridge, Flatiron Health

decision_principles: Role path comes after industry and company strategy so the title reflects the market context.
Bridge roles are valid when they improve industry entry speed and future option value.
Choose roles where the first year can produce visible business impact, not just technical exposure.
role_result.explanation: To navigate the high-risk visa landscape, your strategy shifts from generic job hunting to joining "growth-oriented" firms where your data expertise directly scales their core infrastructure or clinical workflows. By positioning yourself as a "bridge" candidate, you translate your diverse background in healthcare, AI, and SQL-driven reporting into immediate, measurable impact on these companies' operational bottlenecks. Your path success depends on demonstrating "domain fluency" within the first 90 days, effectively proving that your technical skills aren't just academic but are essential drivers of the company's specific strategic goals.

first_month_plan: Map the operating context of Healthcare AI and learn its core metrics.
Build stakeholder understanding around clinical documentation and workflow ai.
Translate job requirements into a 30-day proof-of-value checklist.
Ship one small but visible analysis, dashboard, or workflow improvement that proves execution speed.
Package your strongest matched requirements into interview-ready examples.
month_2_3_plan: Own a recurring analysis or decision-support workflow instead of one-off reporting.
Develop domain fluency so recommendations reflect industry economics, not just data outputs.
Turn one technical capability into a reusable artifact such as a dashboard, model, template, or playbook.
Strengthen cross-functional trust by connecting analysis to a business decision or operational win.
one_year_plan: Become the go-to operator for data analyst problems.
Build a portfolio of impact stories that compound into promotion or stronger external positioning.
Expand from execution into prioritization by influencing what should be measured, automated, or changed.
Create option value for the next move across stronger roles, better companies, or deeper domain specialization.
daily_skill_accumulation: Spend 30-45 minutes strengthening one role-critical skill with a concrete artifact every day.
Maintain a running evidence log of business impact, stakeholder wins, and domain insights.
Refine one resume or narrative bullet each week using fresh evidence from projects.
Read one company, industry, or market note daily to stay macro-aware instead of tool-focused.
value_creation_plan: Target value creation inside Healthcare AI.
Focus on problems close to clinical documentation and workflow ai.
Prefer work that reduces decision latency, improves forecast quality, or creates reusable operational leverage.
Document outcomes in a way that can later power interviews, resumes, and promotion cases.
cover_letter_growth_narrative: I am targeting Healthcare AI because the macro and policy context supports durable demand.
I am especially interested in Abridge because the role appears close to strategic operating problems.
My role path centers on becoming a high-leverage Data Analyst who can translate analysis into decisions.
I want my first year to compound into both immediate contribution and long-term industry positioning.
priority_gaps: Strengthen domain evidence in the chosen industry.
Build one visible project that links analysis to business decisions.
project_recommendations: Build a scoped project that produces one reusable artifact and one quantified business story related to clinical documentation or healthcare workflow efficiency.
job_search_strategy: Bias toward bridge roles such as DA, BI, or analytics-heavy operations paths before stretching too early.
Use the next 4-8 weeks to build one portfolio project and one clearer narrative around business impact.
Treat applications and skill-building as one loop: every gap should inform what you build next.
growth_result.explanation: To align your growth plan with your target, map your past forecasting and clustering achievements directly to the specific clinical or operational bottlenecks highlighted in your target JD, such as patient outcome prediction or workflow optimization. Identify the "evidence gap" by highlighting where your academic research projects—like the Computer Vision lab work—can be framed as proof of your ability to handle complex, unstructured healthcare data. Finally, prioritize near-term execution by building a portfolio piece that solves a simulated version of the company’s core business problem, effectively demonstrating you can deliver immediate value despite your need for visa sponsorship.
